title: penstemon serrulatus
intro: Penstemon serrulatus, also known as Rocky Mountain Penstemon, is a perennial wildflower native to North America, characterized by its clusters of blue to purple flowers and distinctive serrated leaves. The plant has been used in traditional medicine for its antiseptic, anti-inflammatory, and expectorant properties, which can be beneficial in treating respiratory issues such as bronchitis and coughs. The health benefits of Penstemon serrulatus include its ability to soothe sore throats, reduce fever, and alleviate congestion. Herbal preparations of the plant, typically made by infusing the leaves in hot water, can be used as a tea or added to salves and ointments for topical application.
